steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what "leaves" means as used in the passage. Gogol "leaves his signature in fat brush strokes below paintings" — the context is creating art, and the fat brush strokes are the means. The correct answer must describe placing or putting down a visible mark.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Writes: writes his signature in brush strokes — Gogol applies his name physically with paint, and writing fits placing a visible mark. ✓
  • Choice B — Switches: switches means to change or swap; you cannot switch a signature in brush strokes onto a painting. ✗
  • Choice C — Allows: allows means to permit; a signature cannot be permitted in brush strokes — it is created, not allowed. ✗
  • Choice D — Promotes: promotes means to advance or advertise; Gogol is not advertising his name, he is physically marking it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
